title: The Object Type
description: Store grouped data in an object.

slides.title: Grouping Data 📦
slides.content: Rather than creating `let userName` and `let userAge` as separate variables, we often group related data together inside an **Object** using Key-Value pairs.

slides.title: Inline Type Annotation
slides.content: To type an object, we write an annotation that looks identical to an empty JS object, specifying the types of all the keys.

quizData.question: What happens if we forget to provide the 'age' property when defining our `user` object above?

options.text: TypeScript throws an error because the 'age' property is missing.
It prompts the user to input their age.

quizData.explanation: Because we typed it as strict `{ name: string, age: number }`, TS demands that *both* properties exactly match.

slides.title: Extremely Safe 🛡️
slides.content:
If you try to access a property that doesn't exist (like `user.email`), TypeScript will stop you immediately!

It knows exactly what properties the object has based on the annotation.

slides.title: The Messy Syntax 🤢
slides.content:
While inline typing works, writing `{ name: string, age: number, email: string, isActive: boolean }` next to every variable gets very long and hard to read.

There must be a better way... Enter Interfaces!
